video_title: Common Council Regular Meeting, Monday, January 12th 2026

segments.raw_content:
[00:04:32] This is a council meeting session.
[00:04:34] Please rise for the pledge of allegiance
[00:04:40] to the flag of the United States of
[00:04:42] America and to the republic for which it
[00:04:44] stands. One nation under God,
[00:04:47] indivisible, with liberty and justice
[00:04:50] for all.
[00:04:51] >> Please remain standing for the
[00:04:52] invocation.
[00:04:57] >> Thank you.
[00:04:59] >> Esteemed members of the Syracuse Common
[00:05:01] Council, guests and friends, I am
[00:05:03] honored to offer today's invocation. Let
[00:05:05] us pray.
[00:05:07] God of many faiths and many faith
[00:05:09] traditions,
[00:05:11] all wise, full of grace, full of mercy,
[00:05:15] and full of love. As we gather here
[00:05:17] today for the first regular session of
[00:05:19] the common council, let us take a moment
[00:05:21] to pause in gratitude and reflection. We
[00:05:25] are grateful, oh God, for the
[00:05:27] stewardship of our vibrant city.
[00:05:29] Grateful for those who are committed to
[00:05:31] service, to progress, and unity. I pray
[00:05:35] for your divine presence to be with our
[00:05:38] newly elected president Rita Piniagua
[00:05:40] and all members of the common council.
[00:05:42] Their administrative staff as they lead
[00:05:45] with excellence are courageous in the
[00:05:48] face of challenges, discerning in their
[00:05:51] priorities and faithful in their oaths.
[00:05:55] Give them more resiliency, more resolve,
[00:05:57] more patience, and more understanding.
[00:05:59] God, let the distractions this year be
[00:06:01] few and the advancement great. Let them
[00:06:05] serve with willing hands and pure
[00:06:07] hearts. Let their work leave a legacy
[00:06:10] for others to follow and a blueprint
[00:06:13] that's undeniably clear. We ask thy
[00:06:16] blessings, oh God, upon the city of
[00:06:18] Syracuse, its neighbors, its residents,
[00:06:21] and all those who call her home. I
[00:06:24] especially ask your blessings upon the
[00:06:26] most vulnerable within our community,
[00:06:29] our children, our elders, marginalized
[00:06:32] populations, those who are hungry,
[00:06:34] homeless, and home insecure, those in
[00:06:36] need of help and hope. May our city be
[00:06:40] blessed with safety and prosperity and
[00:06:42] harmony. May our decisions serve the
[00:06:44] greater good and foster an environment
[00:06:46] of growth and opportunity.
[00:06:50] Finally, oh God, may the spirit of
[00:06:52] collaboration and respect and purpose
[00:06:55] fill our agendas as much as the business
[00:06:58] of the day. And may each deliberation
[00:07:01] results in making Syracuse whole and
[00:07:04] healthy and hearted. God, may your
[00:07:08] wisdom guide us. May your strength
[00:07:10] search us. May your love compel us. May
[00:07:14] your strength support us. We trust your
[00:07:18] plans. In thy great name we pray. Amen.
[00:07:21] >> Amen.
[00:07:22] >> Amen.
[00:07:32] >> The clerk, please call the role.
[00:07:33] >> Yes, madam president.
[00:07:38] >> Councelor Codwell,
[00:07:39] >> present.
[00:07:39] >> Councelor Micho
[00:07:40] >> here. Council councelor Ray
[00:07:42] >> present.
[00:07:43] >> Councelor Nav
[00:07:44] >> here. Councelor Moore
[00:07:45] >> here.
[00:07:46] >> Councelor Williams here.
[00:07:47] >> Councelor Jones Rouser
[00:07:50] >> present. I'm sorry.
[00:07:51] >> Council MTO
[00:07:52] >> here.
[00:07:53] >> All present.
[00:07:55] >> Thank you. Uh clerk Mcbright. May I have
[00:07:59] have a motion to approve the minutes of
[00:08:02] the
[00:08:04] when was the last meeting?
[00:08:05] >> December 22nd meeting.
[00:08:07] >> So move.
[00:08:08] >> Second. All in favor? I post unanimous.
[00:08:17] Are there any petitions?
[00:08:19] >> No, Madam President.
[00:08:20] >> Are there any committee reports?
[00:08:25] >> No.
[00:08:26] >> Thank you. Are there any communications?
[00:08:28] >> Yes, madam president. Have a few.
[00:08:31] I have one from the honorable Alex Mir
[00:08:34] Mirian, our city auditor, 2025 year
[00:08:37] review report
[00:08:40] from from Evan
[00:08:42] Loving, budget director, the capital
[00:08:45] improvement project. Let's see, I have
[00:08:49] the CRB U quarterly report u from July
[00:08:52] 1st to December 31st last year. I have
[00:08:54] two um letters acknowledging the terms
[00:08:56] and conditions. One of ordinance number
[00:08:58] 32 uh 932 from Syracuse University. The
[00:09:02] other one is 935 from Northland
[00:09:05] Communications.
[00:09:09] >> That's it.
[00:09:10] >> Thank you.
[00:09:11] >> Thank you, counselor.
[00:09:20] >> All right. So moving on into the new
[00:09:22] business
[00:09:24] resolutions approving appointment of
[00:09:26] various persons as commissioners of the
[00:09:29] >> I introduce and urge for his adoption.
[00:09:33] >> All in favor?
[00:09:34] >> I oppose.
[00:09:35] >> Unanimous.
[00:09:36] >> All right, moving on. Councelor Catwell.
[00:09:39] >> Thank you, Madam President. Blessings to

agenda_descriptions:
Agreement — With Elite Gaming, for services related to instructional e-gaming
programs for youth at Wilson Park and Southwest Community Centers, during the
after-school recreation program between January 20 — June 30, 2026.

Amend — Ord. #747 (10/30/2023) “Contract — With Global Traffic Technologies, LLC..,
a division of Miovision Technologies Incorporated, for an Emergency Signal
Preemption system, to provide upgrades to the preemption system, on behalf of the
Department of Public Work in collaboration with the Fire De...

Agreement — With Crouse Health Hospital — HelpPeople Employee Assistance
Program (EAP), to provide Syracuse Firefighters access to counseling, critical
incident support, supervisor consultation, training, and wellness services, for the two
(2) year period of January 1, 2026 — December 31, 2027, with...

Agreement — With Mosaic Consulting Group (RFP #25-266) for Software
Implementation Services for UKG Pro (Human Capital Management and Payroll
Modernization, from the date of execution for the period of eighteen (18) months
expiring March 2027.

Local Law ~ Amend — “Local Law #5 (03/17/2011), of the City of Syracuse to adopt
the NYS Uniform Fire Prevention and Building Code which includes the latest
editions of the following codes: Building, Residential, Existing Building, Fire,
Plumbing, Mechanical, Fuel/Gas, Energy Conservation Constructi...

Sell — All right, title and interest of the City of Syracuse in and to the premises known
as 1521 South Salina Street, a masonry building, to the Greater Syracuse Property
Development Corporation for the total sum of $326 (District 4).

__ escalator, the first year's liability would be $216,150.
Revoke — Ord. #621 (07/01/2024) Permission — (revocable) To Flock Group, Inc.

Special Permit — To establish a “Utility Major’ to establish Battery Energy Storage -
System (BESS) on the property located at 426 East Brighton Avenue. No one spoke
in favor, and no one spoke in opposition to the proposal. Owner, Joseph Tesiero.
Applicant, Terrence Nolan.

Special Permit — To establish a funeral home on the property situated at 4400 South
Salina Street & West Glen Avenue. One (1) person spoke in favor, and one (1)
person spoke in opposition to the proposal. David P. Colegrove, LaBella Associates,
applicant. 4400 South Salina Street LLC, owner.

Advertise Public Hearing — Relative to authorizing a six (6) month moratorium on any
zoning or permit approvals of Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS) within the
City of Syracuse (Public Hearing will be held Monday, January 26, 2026 at 5:30 PM)
